morning_pages.notes: Something clicked. I wrote: 'The book is about unclenching. Not letting go — unclenching. The difference matters. Letting go implies you had something worth having. Unclenching implies you were holding on to nothing, or to something that was hurting your hand.' I think that's it.

writing.notes: Wrote the opening four pages. Not polished. But real. The argument is: we have been taught to approach life as a project to be managed rather than a situation to be inhabited. Aliveness is what returns when the management relaxes. This is genuinely different from Four Thousand Weeks.

imperfectionist.status: Drafting next issue — on the value of incompleteness
imperfectionist.notes: The irony of writing a second newsletter on incompleteness is not lost on me.

family.notes: Good conversation with my wife over dinner. She asked how the book is going. I told her about the 'unclenching' frame. She said it reminded her of something from her novel — a character who can't stop preparing for the scene rather than being in it. We talked for two hours. I washed up.

atelic_activity: Walked the moors for 90 minutes in the afternoon. Found a section I hadn't been to before. Stayed longer than I planned.
note: October 2026 still feels far enough away. Let that be true a little longer.
